interactions.summary: Simon briefly praises Jiang and the host and frames the discussion as speculation that should be tested against events.
Jiang's closing remarks stress that ego, factional struggle, elite insulation, and sheer volatility make the world less rational and less stable than Simon's money-flow model implies.

claims.claim: Jiang argues that political and geopolitical outcomes cannot be reduced to rational monetary calculus because ego, factional rivalry, and short-term interests keep distorting events.
Jiang says even inside transnational or national blocs, only a few factions can win, so internal conflict never disappears just because larger monetary alignments exist.
Jiang predicts that the next few years will bring intense conflict, tension, and unrest because the world is already visibly disgusted with insulated elites.

signature_moments.moment: The world elite appears as a yacht-clustered class floating above a disgusted public.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Jiang uses this image to anchor his claim that public rage and elite insulation are real causal pressures, not just background mood.
